title: ByteWolf Plugin Manifest v0.1
description: What a plugin claims about itself: identity, release version, the capabilities it provides, its dependencies and conflicts, and the capabilities it requests to consume. The manifest is the plugin's claim; the registry decides what it may actually do and records that in a ToolPolicy. The plugin proposes, the registry disposes. See docs/workstreams/plugin-sdk.md.

contract_version.description: Pins the meaning of every field below. A registry that does not know this version must reject the manifest rather than guess.

plugin_id.description: Stable, unique registry key for this plugin, e.g. 'telemetry.read'. Two manifests with the same plugin_id are a conflict the registry rejects.

version.description: The plugin's own release version (semver major.minor.patch). Dependency and conflict resolution compare against this, not against the contract_version.

provides.description: The capabilities this plugin offers. A plugin that provides nothing has no reason to be registered, so at least one is required.

requires.description: Other plugins this one depends on. A missing or circular dependency blocks the whole load; the registry never starts a plugin with an unmet requirement.

version_range.description: A constraint on the dependency's release version, e.g. '>=1.0,<2.0'. The schema keeps it a non-empty string; the registry parses and enforces the range.

conflicts.description: plugin_ids or capability_ids this plugin cannot coexist with. Registering it while a conflicting entry is present is rejected.

requests.description: Capabilities this plugin asks to CONSUME from others. This is a request, not a grant: the registry intersects it with the allowlist and emits a fail-closed ToolPolicy.

reloadable.description: Whether the registry may hot-reload this plugin in place (register -> start -> reload -> ... -> stop) without a full restart. Defaults to false when absent: a plugin is not reloadable unless it says so.

state_migration.description: On reload, whether in-memory state is dropped ('none') or carried across ('preserve'). Only meaningful for a reloadable plugin.

then.description: state_migration describes reload behaviour, so it may not appear on a plugin that is not reloadable.

capability.description: Embedded Capability. Kept structurally identical to capability_v0_1.schema.json; a test asserts every provided capability also validates against that standalone schema, so the two cannot drift.
